cards.question: Page 8 in 'About this guide' (Contacting Infor) - detail 1: Explain 'About this guide Use this guide' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: About this guide Use this guide as a reference for industry-specific and role-based content for core and industry applications that are available through standard provisioning in this CloudSuite. For information about the offered industry content types, see the product documentation references that are cited throughout this document. Intended audience This guide is for role-based users of this CloudSuite.

cards.question: Page 8 in 'About this guide' (Contacting Infor) - detail 2: Explain 'Related documents Related documentation can be' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Related documents Related documentation can be found in the CloudSuite Food and Beverage Documentation Library. Contacting Infor If you have questions about Infor products or documentation, go to Infor Concierge at https:// concierge.infor.com/ and create a support incident. For the latest documentation, go to Documentation Central at docs.infor.com.

cards.question: Page 8 in 'About this guide' (Contacting Infor) - detail 3: Explain 'We recommend that you check this' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: We recommend that you check this website periodically for updated documentation. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | About this guide

cards.question: Page 9 in 'Overview' (Widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 1: Overview Industry content is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 1: Overview Industry content is the usage of Infor technologies to provide options and solutions that make our cloudsuites rich in functionality in the context of a business domain. These industry content types are available for the core applications and industry applications of CloudSuite Food and Beverage: • Workspaces • Widgets • H5 Scripts • Experience Designer • Industry Process Catalog process materials and preconfigured data • Industry Analytics Other industry content types are available in Infor Marketplace. Role-based workspaces Workspaces contain widgets and in-context applications to help you perform tasks and resolve issues that are related to your role. These role-based workspaces are readily available in the OS Workspace Catalog and are updated automatically when enhancements occur.

cards.question: Page 9 in 'Overview' (Widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Optionally, you can copy a role-based' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Optionally, you can copy a role-based workspace to use it as basis for creating your own private page. You can then select additional widgets from the Widget Catalog. Note: Updates to role-based workspaces are not applied to copied workspaces. To monitor workspaces for updates, see the last edited date for the workspace in the Workspace Catalog.

cards.question: Page 9 in 'Overview' (Widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'For more information about workspaces, see' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For more information about workspaces, see the Infor OS User and Administration Documentation Library (Cloud). Widgets Widgets are small, single-purpose applications that provide at-a-glance information and access to some interactive functions. They are provided within the Infor OS Widget Catalog for use on both standard and Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Overview

cards.question: Page 10 in 'Overview' (Industry analytics) - detail 1: Explain 'role-based workspaces. Most widgets are configurable' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: role-based workspaces. Most widgets are configurable and can be modified to meet specific situational needs. They are also updated automatically when enhancements are made. Widgets that are built for specific tasks related to your role are available in a role-based workspace. Optionally, you can add more widgets from the widget catalog to your workspace. H5 script A script is one of the personalization tools in H5 that you can use to enhance the functionality of standard M3 programs based on your preference. Experience Designer M3 Experience Designer is a tool embedded in M3 H5, where you can build and customize applications based on your requirements.

cards.question: Page 10 in 'Overview' (Industry analytics) - detail 2: Explain 'You can add and configure components' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You can add and configure components on the application, such as index list, form, tab, data grid, application header, details header, and media. Each component is configured individually and you can configure how components interact with each other. M3 APIs are used to supply data to each component on the application. Note: To use Experience Designer, you must use the latest version of H5. If you are using the H5 Classic, then the designer tool is not available from the M3 menu and you cannot run the applications that are created in the designer tool. Industry Process Catalog The Infor Industry Process Catalog is a streamlined implementation methodology that provides preconfigured data and process materials to integrate your core industry processes. Industry Process Catalog process descriptions can be accessed on Infor Concierge.

cards.question: Page 10 in 'Overview' (Industry analytics) - detail 3: Explain 'These descriptions are automatically updated in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: These descriptions are automatically updated in Process Intelligence. Preconfigured data is part of standard provisioning. To access this solution, navigate to Infor OS Portal and select Process Intelligence. Industry analytics Industry Analytics are embedded, cloud-based, industry-specific analytics content. This content provides support for data-driven decisions and access to business-critical insights across the CloudSuite. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Overview

cards.question: Page 11 in 'Overview' (Industry analytics) - detail 1: Explain 'See the M3 Analytics on page' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: See the M3 Analytics on page section for an overview of the content.

cards.question: Page 11 in 'Overview' (Industry analytics) - detail 2: Explain 'For detailed information, see the Infor' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For detailed information, see the Infor M3 Analytics for CloudSuite Food and Beverage Product Guide (Cloud).

cards.question: Page 11 in 'Overview' (Industry analytics) - detail 3: Explain 'Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Overview

cards.question: Page 12 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Payable Controller Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 2: Role-based workspaces These role-based' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 2: Role-based workspaces These role-based workspaces are available for your CloudSuite: • Accounts Payable Controller Workspace • Accounts Receivable Controller Workspace • Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Workspace • Customer Service Representative Workspace • Financial Accounting • Financial Controlling • General Ledger Controller Workspace • Printer Programs Utility page • Production Manager Workspace • Production Planner • Purchase Manager Workspace • Quality Manager Workspace • Systems Manager • VP Production Workspace • VP Sales Workspace • VP Supply Chain Workspace • Warehouse Dispatcher • Warehouse Manager Workspace • Warehouse Receiver Accounts Payable Controller Workspace The accounts payable controller manages a team of accounts payable clerks. Together, they manage the financial relationship with suppliers; ensuring timely and accurate payments, monitoring financial health, and maintaining good relationships with them.

cards.question: Page 12 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Payable Controller Workspace) - detail 2: Explain 'This role focuses on helping new' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This role focuses on helping new suppliers assess their financial statements regularly. An accounts payable controller prepares the analysis of accounts and produces monthly reports to ensure that the executive team has a clear picture of liabilities from which they can make investment decisions. This role investigates ways to streamline the payment process, for example reducing manual steps, to ensure invoices and queries are processed timely without requiring additional headcount.

cards.question: Page 12 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Payable Controller Workspace) - detail 3: Explain 'Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Role-based workspaces

cards.question: Page 13 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Payable Controller Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'This workspace contains these widgets: DescriptionWidget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This workspace contains these widgets: DescriptionWidget Displays the current date, greeting message, and the user's icon with first name.My Day Presents the top suppliers with the most number of invoices specific to a preconfigured period, for example past days from the current date. Hover over a bar line to show the total value of invoices and the name of the supplier in a tooltip. You can click a bar line to drill down. On the records page, right-click a record and select Open to perform a drillback to M3. See M3 Information Viewer. Top Suppliers Focuses on managing priority tasks. This widget contains matrices that open card tasks in the Required Actions widget: • Invoices for review: Displays invoices that are pending for review for the current user where due date is either set as past days or the next additional days from the current date. • Invoices due for payment: Displays invoices that are approved and pending for payment wher

cards.question: Page 13 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Payable Controller Workspace) - detail 2: Explain '• New suppliers: Displays all suppliers' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • New suppliers: Displays all suppliers tagged with status '10-Preliminary' that are created in the past days. • Stopped invoices: Shows the number of invoices that are stopped for payment where due date is either set as past days or the next additional days from the current date. For every card task, click Review to view a breakdown of records and identify tasks to address. Optionally, you may hide a card task by clicking the bell icon. When you review the available records, you may access these additional functionality through clicking Review or View All: • Select all or any records to export. Click Export to CSV. • Drill back into an M3 program by clicking a record with a working link. • Invoices for review, invoices due for payment, and stopped invoices matrices redirects to 'Acc Payable.

cards.question: Page 13 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Payable Controller Workspace) - detail 3: Explain 'Display' (APS200). • New suppliers matrix' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Display' (APS200). • New suppliers matrix redirects to 'Customer. Open' (CRS610). • Search for a particular record. On the Required Actions widget, clicking View All provides you a single view of all records in tabular format. Whereas, clicking View snooze list enables you to show the list of hidden card tasks. From the list, you can return a hidden card task on the main widget display by clicking Unsnooze. On Your Radar Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Role-based workspaces

cards.question: Page 14 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Payable Controller Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'DescriptionWidget Displays all tasks that you' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: DescriptionWidget Displays all tasks that you perform frequently. This widget shows these default tasks: • Update Supplier Information: Shows the list of suppliers where you can update supplier details by selecting a particular record. After saving the record, a message is displayed with the supplier ID which you can click to perform a drillback to 'Supplier. Open' (CRS620). • Supplier List Information: Displays a list of supplier contact information that you can use to connect to an active supplier. See Supplier List.

cards.question: Page 14 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Payable Controller Workspace) - detail 2: Explain '• Supplier Creation: Provides a form' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Supplier Creation: Provides a form for adding a supplier data into M3. See Supplier Creation. Quick Actions Provides direct access to preconfigured programs in the Infor M3 application. This widget shows default links that drill back to M3 where you can view more details. See menu widget in the Infor OS User and Administration Documentation Library. Important Links Use this widget to list down all items in M3.

cards.question: Page 14 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Payable Controller Workspace) - detail 3: Explain 'See Item Details. Item Details Displays' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: See Item Details. Item Details Displays the alternate suppliers available with their corresponding agreements based on your selected item. See Alternate Supplier Visibility. Alternate Supplier Visibility APIs used in the workspace widgets This table shows the APIs used in varying widgets for this workspace: TransactionProgramWidget SearchSupInvoiceAPS200MITop Suppliers SearchSupInvoicAPS200MIOn Your Radar Invoices for review SearchSupInvoicAPS200MIOn Your Radar Invoices due for payment SearchSupplierCRS620MIOn Your Radar New supplier SearchSupInvoicAPS200MIOn Your Radar Stopped invoices SearchSupplierCRS620MIQuick Actions Update Supplier Information Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Role-based workspaces

cards.question: Page 15 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Receivable Controller Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'TransactionProgramWidget GetBasicDataCRS620MIQuick Actions Update Supplier Information' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: TransactionProgramWidget GetBasicDataCRS620MIQuick Actions Update Supplier Information UpdSupplierCRS620MIQuick Actions Update Supplier Information LstVatCRS030MIQuick Actions Update Supplier Information LstByCodeCRS650MIQuick Actions Update Supplier Information GetUserDataMNS150MIQuick Actions Update Supplier Information Programs and sorting orders used in the workspace The drillback functionality in some of the workspace widgets require specific configuration for filtering the correct data in Infor M3. cards.question: Page 15 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Receivable Controller Workspace) - detail 2: Explain 'This table shows the programs with' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This table shows the programs with their corresponding sorting order and table information all of which should be in active status: TableSorting orderProgram FPLEDG1APS200 FPLEDG2APS200 CIDMAS1CRS620 Accounts Receivable Controller Workspace The Accounts Receivable Controller can use this workspace to manage all aspects of the invoice lifecycle and credit control, from the shipment of goods to the receipt of payment. Within this workspace, reminder letters are generated at various escalation levels and reviewed before being issued to customers as required. Invoices placed on hold or stop are displayed in a unified view across all customers, alongside Tickler Notes that document customer interactions, enabling faster resolution of reported issues. Credit tracking is also available, showing the remaining credit available to each customer in a graphical format with drill-down capabilities for detailed insights.

cards.question: Page 15 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Receivable Controller Workspace) - detail 3: Explain 'This monitoring supports proactive account management' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This monitoring supports proactive account management, helping prevent customers from exceeding their credit limits. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Role-based workspaces

cards.question: Page 16 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Receivable Controller Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'Additionally, the average time that customers' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Additionally, the average time that customers take to pay invoices is calculated in the Days Outstanding view. This view serves as a valuable indicator of potential issues and aids in cash flow management for more effective oversight of customer debt. This table shows the available widgets in the workspace: DescriptionWidget Shows the current date, greeting message, and the user's icon with first name.My Day Shows a three-dimensional bubble chart illustrating the relationship among the number of write-off invoices, generated invoices, and the percentage of write-offs relative to total order invoices. The bubble size represents the proportion of write- offs. Hover over a bubble chart to show the total values in a tooltip. Write-Off Analysis Shows a three dimensional chart illustrating the relationship between payers’ stopped amount percentage, overdue amount percentage, and DSO value. Hover over a bubble chart to show the total values in

cards.goodAnswer: This widget contains matrices that open card tasks in the Required Actions widget: • Payment reminders: Shows the total count of outstanding invoices that are grouped based on the number of payment reminders set for each invoice using the RMQT field and stopped invoices. • Review AR balances and aged invoices: Shows the total count of invoices sent within the last thirty days, invoices overdue by up to thirty days from the current date, open invoices aged between thirty and ninety days, and high-risk invoices older than ninety days. • Open credit notes: Shows the total count of open credit notes. • Customer data - action required: Shows the total count of customers that are tagged with status 10, customers with credit blocked value between and 3, customer invoices with stopped payment reminders, and tickler notes for each payee. The tickler note may contain action items, reminders, or brief notes from customer discussions. For every car

cards.goodAnswer: • Drill back into an M3 program: Click a record with a working link. • Search for a record. cards.question: Page 17 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Accounts Receivable Controller Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'DescriptionWidget Shows tasks that you perform' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: DescriptionWidget Shows tasks that you perform frequently. This widget shows these default tasks: • Update tickler information: Shows the list of customers with available tickler notes or customer feedback that you can update. • Update customer information: Shows a list of customers along with their corre- sponding details, which you can update. The customer details in this workspace are related to finance or general ledger information such as VAT values, cash discount terms, and credit limit. After saving the record, a message containing the customer ID is displayed. You can click this ID to drill back into M3, where the customer record is stored. • Where am I working: Checks in at your current work location.

cards.question: Page 20 in 'Role-based workspaces' (CFO Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'CFO Workspace The Chief Financial Officer' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: CFO Workspace The Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Workspace contains key financial insights, with a focus on factors that can impact the organization’s financial health and operational efficiency. This workspace is for senior financial leaders who need a high-level visibility into key financial metrics and trends to identify risks and support strategic financial planning. This workspace shows these key ratios that are sourced from the current M3 Analytics solution: • Profitability • Liquidity • Efficiency Note: To show data in the widgets, ensure that M3 Analytics is set up and running. See Infor M3 Analytics for M3 Industry CloudSuites Administration Guide and select Setup and configuration in M3 > Finance Key Ratio.

cards.question: Page 20 in 'Role-based workspaces' (CFO Workspace) - detail 2: Explain 'This workspace contains these widgets: DescriptionWidget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

Gross Margin Shows the recorded amount for EBITDA report line.EBITDA Shows the Recorded Amt for Revenues report line.Return on Equity Shows a liquidity ratio that measures the ability to cover the com- pany’s short-term obligations and debt with its current assets. Cash Conversion Cycle Customer Service Representative Workspace The Customer Service Representative (CSR) is part of a dedicated team led by the Customer Service Manager, playing a vital role in the organization’s commitment to exceptional customer service.

cards.question: Page 21 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Customer Service Representative Workspace) - detail 2: Explain 'As a front-line support professional, the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: As a front-line support professional, the CSR addresses customer inquiries and concerns with the primary goal of ensuring a positive and satisfying experience. This table shows the available widgets in the workspace: DescriptionWidget Shows the current date, a greeting message, and the user's icon and first name. My Day Shows line amounts alongside customer order stop codes to help users identify the most common reasons for stopped orders and gain better insight into planned open orders. The report covers a rolling 12-month period. The Open Sales Orders Highlights top payers based on line amounts to enhance visibility into payer-related order stoppages.

cards.question: Page 21 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Customer Service Representative Workspace) - detail 3: Explain 'These updates addressed previous gaps in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: These updates addressed previous gaps in analyzing order stoppages and monitoring payer impact. The report reflects data for the current period. Stopped Sales Orders by Payer Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Role-based workspaces

cards.question: Page 22 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Customer Service Representative Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'DescriptionWidget Focuses on managing priority tasks' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: DescriptionWidget Focuses on managing priority tasks. This widget contains these matrices that open card tasks in the Re- quired Actions widget: • All delayed order lines: Shows customer order lines which are past the target departure date, for example, past days from the current date. When you review all delayed order lines, you can change and save the planning date for a specific customer order. Under the New planning date, specify a new date and click the Confirm Planning date icon. To update the list of records, you must close the task and select Refresh from the widget menu. cards.question: Page 41 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Purchase Manager Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'DescriptionWidget Focuses on managing priority tasks' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: DescriptionWidget Focuses on managing priority tasks. This widget contains matrices that open card tasks in the Required Actions widget: • Delayed order lines: Shows all purchase order lines for which the precon- figured dates are past the target delivery date, for example, past days to the current date. When you review delayed order lines, you can change and save the planning date for a specific purchase order number. Under the New planning date, specify a new date and click the Confirm Planning date icon. To update the list of records, you must close the task and select Refresh from the widget menu. The updated order line is removed after you supply a target date in the future. • PO approvals: Shows purchase orders set with status '12=Order stopped' and with dates set to the previous days from the current date or days later. When you review PO approvals, you can approve a specific purchase order by clicking Approve PO. To update the l

cards.question: Page 41 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Purchase Manager Workspace) - detail 2: Explain 'The approved order line changes its' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The approved order line changes its status to '15=Ready for printout' and is removed from the list of records. For some records, you must specify a signature under the Signature column before you can proceed with the approval process. The signature usually consists of a two-letter code assigned to an additional approver. • New suppliers: Shows new suppliers tagged with the status '10-Prelimi- nary'. You can approve new suppliers by clicking Approve Supplier, which trig- gers the specific supplier to change status to '20-Approved'. To update the list of records, you must close the task and select Refresh from the widget menu. The approved supplier is removed from the preliminary list. Additionally, you can add an address for a specific supplier by clicking Add Address and providing the required information to complete the address. You can access the updated address in M3 through (CRS622).

cards.question: Page 41 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Purchase Manager Workspace) - detail 3: Explain '• Stopped suppliers: Shows all suppliers' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Stopped suppliers: Shows all suppliers tagged with status '30-Stopped'. You can activate stopped suppliers by clicking Approve Supplier, which triggers the specific supplier to change status to '20-Approved'. To update the list of records, you must close the task and select Refresh from the widget menu. The approved supplier is removed from the preliminary list. Additionally, you can add an address for a specific supplier by clicking Add Address and providing the required information to complete the address. You can access the updated address in M3 through (CRS622). For every card task, click Review to view a breakdown of records and identify tasks to address. Optionally, you can hide a card task by clicking the bell icon. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Role-based workspaces

cards.question: Page 55 in 'Role-based workspaces' (VP Supply Chain Workspace) - detail 1: Explain 'VP Supply Chain Workspace The VP' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: VP Supply Chain Workspace The VP Supply Chain Workspace provides high-level, actionable insights across fulfillment performance, procurement efficiency, inventory control, and supplier reliability. This workspace is built on existing M3 Analytics and Birst data models, leveraging available content to deliver meaningful visualizations through Analytics Widgets. This list shows the main focus areas of this workspace: • Ensure sustainable and cost-efficient supply across priority product categories and high-value customer segments. • Monitor fulfillment performance, procurement trends, and alignment with forecasted demand. • Identify drivers of margin erosion across the supply chain, such as rising input costs, inefficiencies, or supplier issues.

cards.question: Page 57 in 'Role-based workspaces' (Warehouse Dispatcher) - detail 1: Explain 'Warehouse Dispatcher The Warehouse Dispatcher specializes' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Warehouse Dispatcher The Warehouse Dispatcher specializes in order fulfillment and logistics coordination. This role ensures the accurate, timely, and efficient dispatch of goods while maintaining strong communication with carriers and internal teams. By implementing best practices in shipment tracking, documentation, and compliance, the Warehouse Dispatcher upholds transparency and ethical standards in operations. Additionally, they contribute to the organization’s sustainability goals by optimizing delivery routes and reducing waste. The goal is to support the success of the supply chain by ensuring smooth outbound operations, timely deliveries, and overall efficiency and profitability. This workspace contains these widgets: DescriptionWidget Displays the current date, greeting message, and the user's icon and first name.My Day Displays the customer order delivery in the past days for the current user.

On the Required Actions widget, clicking View All provides you a single view of all records in tabular format. Whereas, clicking View snooze list enables you to show the list of hidden card tasks. From the list, you can return a hidden card task on the main widget display by clicking Unsnooze. On Your Radar Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Role-based workspaces

cards.question: Page 87 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Configuring the Customer Address widget The' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Configuring the Customer Address widget The default view shows all types of customer addresses. To change the view or modify the widget settings, follow this procedure. Click the menu icon (…) on the widget title bar and select Configure. Specify this information: Title Click the unlock button and specify a widget title. Address type Select the purpose of your customized address based on the available address types. Address line Select the address line information to show in the list view. Show temporary addresses Enable this toggle to show temporary addresses in the widget. A temporary address usually starts with a number sign. Show inactive addresses Enable this toggle to show inactive, past addresses in the widget.

cards.question: Page 87 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Click Save. Customer Contacts Use this' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Click Save. Customer Contacts Use this widget to get the contact information of customers who are registered in M3 through 'Customer. Open' (CRS610). When you access the widget, it displays records linked to the logged-in user. You can browse through the customers, each showing their corresponding number, name, and associated contact information. Note: Before you use the widget, consider this information: • Data to be loaded on this widget is user-specific. The widget accepts the login user ID by default and retrieves customer record registered in M3 through 'Customer. Open' (CRS610) where the login user ID has been mapped as a responsible or a sales person. • You can override the logged-in user ID in the configuration section of the widget to retrieve the customer record of another user ID in place of the logged-in user.

cards.question: Page 87 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain '• You can filter by contact' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • You can filter by contact type as defined in 'Contact Type. Open' (CRS621) where the widget retrieves customer records mapped with the appropriate contact type in 'Customer. Connect Contact' (CMS055) to get a specific customer and its corresponding contact details. You can perform these tasks in the widget: • Search for a specific customer in the widget using a user ID or name. The search results follow the applied contact type and customer status filters based on the widget configuration. • Click the email address of the customer to open an email application available in the user system to send email to the customer's mail address. • Click the customer phone number to make outgoing calls to the customer number. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

Prerequisites • Before using the widget, you must activate these tables in the M3 Function Search administration: • OCUSMA • CMNUSR • MITWHL • OCUSCO • CEMAIL • CCUCON See information about updating table indexes in the M3 Core User and Administration Library (Cloud). • To use the outgoing call feature of the widget, you must have a calling feature-enabled application that is available in your system, for example, Microsoft Teams and other related applications.

cards.question: Page 90 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'You can select a customer from' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You can select a customer from either the Customer Contacts widget or from 'Customer. Open' (CRS610) in Infor M3. When you select a customer, the available equipment is displayed in the Customer Equipment List widget. The list view shows the equipment's item number, serial number, registration number, and meter reading. You can see the ownership type and additional details for every piece of equipment. You can filter the ownership types and click the ownership badge connected to a drillback that opens 'Equipment/Serialized Item. Open' (MMS240). When you expand the equipment information, you can view additional details with the corresponding number of records returned by the API transactions.

Open' (MOS500) Prerequisites • Before using the widget, you must activate these tables in the M3 Function Search administration: • MILOIN • MITMAS • ADECPL • MOOPGM See information about updating table indexes in the M3 Core User and Administration Library (Cloud). • Ensure that the Customer Contacts widget is added along with the Customer Address widget from the widget catalog on your workspace. cards.question: Page 93 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Customer Return Use this widget to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Customer Return Use this widget to initiate customer returns using negative customer order lines by searching relevant orders based on specific categories: • Order number • Customer's order number • Delivery number • Invoice number • Customer or item or both You can also do manual return in the widget by specifying the appropriate customer and item number. Note: This widget does not utilize customer return in 'Customer Return. Open' (OIS390) but creates a customer order line with a negative quantity. Widget alerts • For non-returnable items: You can process returns only for items marked as '0-Returnable' on 'Item. Open' (MMS001/F). If you attempt to return an item labeled as non-returnable on (MMS001/F) and click Next, the widget prevents this action and displays an appropriate alert message. • For different customer orders selection: In the Select order line window, if you choose order lines from a different customer than the original

cards.question: Page 93 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (OIS010/G) is set to either' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (OIS010/G) is set to either '1-Check' or '2-Check and block', the widget displays a relevant alert if you select multiple lines with the same item number. Searching multiple items for return In the Search Order field, click and select the category to be used for the search. Provide the information based on your selected category. For example, selecting Order no from the list requires you to provide the exact order number. If you select Cust/Item no, you can select either the customer or item number or both in their respective fields. Click Search. In the Select Order Lines window, the widget retrieves customer order lines that match the search criteria. You can view details, such as customer number, order number, facility, and customer order number to manually select the appropriate order lines.

cards.question: Page 93 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Click Next. In the Customer Order' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Click Next. In the Customer Order Lines window, view your selected item together with the order number and the return price per quantity. Additionally, specify this required information: Return quantity The maximum amount for this field is based on the available order quantity. The widget by default returns the order quantity as return quantity, but you can edit the return quantity based on your need. Select Lot Number The widget retrieves all the lot numbers that are applicable to the specific order number. Select the appropriate lot number for the item from the list. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 94 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Note: If item is not lot' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note: If item is not lot controlled, this field is disabled. Select Reason Code The widget retrieves the possible reason code defined in 'Transaction Reason. Open' (CRS103) as a list. Select the appropriate reason for the return. Click Add to checkout. Repeat the steps to add more items for return. After adding all the items, click the Checkout link in the widget. In the Customer Order Lines window, validate the list of items for return. Click Create return. After the widget successfully creates the return order, click the generated order number to drill back to 'Customer Order. Open Toolbox' (OIS300/B) and view the customer order that is filtered in M3.

cards.question: Page 94 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Creating a manual return Click Manual' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Creating a manual return Click Manual return. In the Manual Return window, specify this information: Customer number Specify the customer number from whom the items are being returned. Reference number Specify the reference number of the customer order. Item number Specify the identification number of the item. Quantity Specify the quantity of the items to return. Click Get item price per quantity. The Return price field with the corresponding price for the item is displayed. Alternatively, you can modify the return price value. Click + Add line. The item details that you provided are added to the list. The widget retrieves all the lot numbers that are applicable to the specific order number.

cards.question: Page 94 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'In the Select Lot Number field' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: In the Select Lot Number field, select the appropriate lot number for the item from the list. Note: If item is not lot controlled, this field is disabled. The widget retrieves the possible reason code that are defined in (CRS103) as a list. In the Select Reason field, select the appropriate reason for the return. Click Create return. After the widget successfully creates the return order, click the generated order number to drill back to 'Customer Order. Open Toolbox' (OIS300/B) and view the customer order that is filtered in M3. Configuring the Customer Return widget Click Configure. Specify this information: Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.goodAnswer: Drillback Actions Use this widget to create a list of drillback actions, which you can click to redirect to an M3 program or an external link. You can add these types of links: • Static: Contains a permanent value • Dynamic: Changes when the widget receives data from context messages This widget receives various context messages. You can use this with any M3 program, any workspace widget, or any application that sends a context message such as Infor Business Context and Infor Widget Context. Additionally, you can perform these actions: • Show the latest drillbacks only • Drag a drillback action to reorder the list • Edit a drillback action • Delete a drillback action Prerequisite Before using the widget, you must have access to Workspaces and knowledge in Drillback URLs. cards.question: Page 97 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'URL Specify the drillback URL. For' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: URL Specify the drillback URL. For programs in M3, copy the drillback URL from the Context Viewer widget. Note: Before specifying the URL, change the dynamic parameter to its property key enclosed in {}. The Context Viewer shows all available elements for the M3 program and this includes the property key with its dynamic parameter. You must identify the dynamic parameter in the URL which is shown in this example:LogicalId=lid://infor.m3.m3&program=CRS620&tableName=CIDMAS&keys=IDCONO%2C770%2CID SUNO%2C100375%2B%2B%2B%2B&option=5&name=CRS620%2FE&description=Supplier.%20Open&panel=E" The character 100375 is a dynamic parameter, therefore you must change it to {id1} as its property key. This example shows the correct format of the URL to specify in the URL field: LogicalId=lid://infor.m3.m3&program=CRS620&tableName=CIDMAS&keys=IDCONO%2C770%2CID SUNO%2C{id1}%2B%2B%2B%2B&option=5&name=CRS620%2FE&description=Supplier.%20Open&panel=E Optionally

cards.question: Page 97 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'The various parameters in the summary' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The various parameters in the summary include an overview that covers the unique identification, brand, age, location, and the owner of the equipment, health status, equipment run time based on meter readings, service history, service agreement, service report detailing its latest inspections, and summary based on performance tests. The content may still require verification to ensure the accuracy of AI-generated responses. Regular equipment health checks contribute to the reliability and safety of heavy machinery, minimizing the risk of unexpected breakdowns and maximizing operational efficiency. Keeping the latest summary of regular health checks helps the service customer support provide customers with accurate status and quick updates on their equipment. The summary is also saved in M3 as a text block in 'Equipment/Serialized Item. Open' (MMS240). Prerequisites • This widget works within M3 if the application or program is sending I

cards.question: Page 97 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain '• When you use this widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

For example, you may open the M3 Information Viewer widget to retrieve equipment listed from 'Work Schedule. Open Toolbox' (MOS195). Generating a health check summary This procedure is applicable when your project holds an empty summary. In M3, select an equipment from 'Work Order. Open' (MOS100). Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 98 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'If used in a workspace, click' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If used in a workspace, click an equipment from the M3 Information Viewer widget. On the widget, click Generate. The widget opens an Equipment Health pop up where the generation of the new summary takes place. You can modify the health summary from the widget body if necessary or click Regenerate to produce a new content of health summary based on your selected equipment. If your summary requires to be translated, select an available language from the Language field. The summary might take awhile to translate. Click Save. The summary is saved in your selected language. Translating a health check summary in all available languages Use this procedure to translate your health summary in all languages available in OS Portal. All translated summaries are stored in text blocks which you can view through 'Equipment/Serialized Item.

cards.question: Page 98 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (MMS240/B). In M3, select an' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MMS240/B). In M3, select an equipment from 'Work Order. Open' (MOS100). If used in a workspace, click an equipment from the M3 Information Viewer widget. On the widget menu, click Configure. From the Settings dialog, switch Save in all languages. Click Save. Return to the main widget display and click Generate all. The widget opens an Equipment Health pop up but containing all available languages alongside with the summary. Generation of translated content takes awhile to complete.

You may also copy a translated content and paste it in a different file format to use for review. Click Save. Save in all language Switch this toggle to generate your health summary in all available languages.. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 99 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Click Save. Image Digitization Use this' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Click Save. Image Digitization Use this widget to extract information from an external PDF document or image into an M3 program using Infor GenAI. You can configure this widget to use features that support different use cases. This table shows the available features in the Image Digitization widget: DescriptionFeature Retrieves item information and inserts it into an open customer order in M3. Customer Orders Retrieves supplier invoice information and creates a new supplier in- voice record in M3.

cards.question: Page 99 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Supplier Invoice Customer Orders You can' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Supplier Invoice Customer Orders You can retrieve item information from PDF documents and images and add the item information into an open customer order in M3. Note: The documents and images are automatically uploaded to IDM with the CS_Orders document type. See the default attributes used for the CS_Orders document type in IDM in Document attribute mapping for IDM on page 101. Prerequisites Before using the Customer Orders feature, you must enable the M3_H5_ItemsFromPDFHelper script on Customer Order. Open Line (OIS101).

cards.question: Page 99 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Limitations The Customer Orders feature is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Limitations The Customer Orders feature is only available for the Customer Order. Open Line (OIS101) M3 program. Infor Business Context (IBC) message The Customer Orders feature receives these Infor Business Context (IBC) messages from M3: • InforCustomerPartyMaster • InforSalesOrder Adding item information using the Customer Orders feature Drag and drop a file to the widget. Alternatively, click upload files. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 100 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Note: You can also click the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note: You can also click the menu icon on the widget title bar and select Paste image from clipboard to upload a file to the widget. Additionally, the call to Infor GenAI is set to a maximum of response tokens. Large files may not be processed correctly in the widget. You must verify the generated results. Optionally, click Replace to modify the retrieved item description in the widget. a In the Select item dialog box, specify the item name, then click Search. b Optionally, click Reversed Search to search for results based on the reversed word order of the search string. c Select an item from the search results page, then click Select.

cards.question: Page 100 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Optionally, specify an item quantity for' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Optionally, specify an item quantity for the retrieved item description. Click Save. Configuring the Customer Orders feature in the Image Digitization widget Click Configure. Specify this information: Title Click the unlock button and specify a widget title. Feature Select Customer Orders. CMS030: Key search ID (optional) Optionally, specify a field ID to replace the default search query. Use decimal for quantities Enable this setting to show decimal places in the quantity field of the widget. OIS345: Price check Enable this setting to show a comparison between the retrieved item price and the item price in M3 Business Engine.

cards.question: Page 100 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'OIS345: Gross or net price Select' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: OIS345: Gross or net price Select the price to use for price comparisons. Click Save. Supplier Invoice You can retrieve supplier invoice information from PDF documents and images and create a new supplier invoice entry in M3. Note: The documents and images are automatically uploaded to IDM with the M3_SupplierInvoice document type. See the default attributes used for the M3_SupplierInvoice document type in IDM in Document attribute mapping for IDM on page 101. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 101 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Prerequisites Before using this feature, you' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Prerequisites Before using this feature, you must ensure that these requirements are met: • The purchase order number and items in the uploaded file are existing entries in M3. • The uploaded supplier invoice record does not exist in Supplier Invoice Batch Open (APS450). Creating a supplier invoice record using the Supplier Invoice feature Drag and drop a file to the widget. Alternatively, click upload files. Note: You can click the menu icon on the widget title bar and select Paste image from clipboard to upload a file to the widget. Additionally, the call to Infor GenAI is set to a maximum of response tokens. Large files may not be processed correctly in the widget.

cards.question: Page 101 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'You must verify the generated results' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You must verify the generated results. Verify the information retrieved from the uploaded file. Optionally, you can modify the retrieved information or specify new information in the widget interface. Click Create. Configuring the Supplier Invoice feature in the Image Digitization widget Click Configure. Specify this information: Title Click the unlock button and specify a widget title. Feature Select Supplier Invoice.

cards.question: Page 101 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Disable manual approval step Enable this' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Disable manual approval step Enable this setting to automatically create a supplier invoice after the data is retrieved from PDF documents and images. Disable APS450 drillback Enable this setting to prevent the drillback to APS450 after you create a supplier invoice. Use a user-defined IDM document type Enable this setting to set a custom IDM document type for files uploaded to the widget. Note: The list of available IDM document types and attributes in this setting depends on the documents uploaded to IDM and your role in IDM. Click Save. Document attribute mapping for IDM This table shows the default attributes used in documents and images for each document type uploaded to IDM through the Image Digitization widget: Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 102 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'AttributeDocument type M3_Order_NumberCS_Orders M3_CustomerCS_Orders M3_CompanyCS_Orders M3_DivisionCS_Orders' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: AttributeDocument type M3_Order_NumberCS_Orders M3_CustomerCS_Orders M3_CompanyCS_Orders M3_DivisionCS_Orders M3_AccountingEntityCS_Orders BOD_SupplierInvoiceIDM3_SupplierInvoice BOD_AccountingEntityIDM3_SupplierInvoice M3_InvoiceDateM3_SupplierInvoice M3_CompanyM3_SupplierInvoice M3_DivisionM3_SupplierInvoice BOD_RemitToPartyIDM3_SupplierInvoice BOD_SupplierPartyIDM3_SupplierInvoice M3_InvoiceYearM3_SupplierInvoice In-Context Information Use this widget to show M3 data in a chart format. These types of charts are available: • Area: A display of quantitative data connected by line segments and the area below the line is covered with color. • Bar: A horizontal comparison of data through the use of columns. • Column: A vertical comparison of data that shares the same category or label. • Count: A numeric display of data wherein a set of digits represent the count or amount of a specific value.

cards.question: Page 102 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain '• Donut: A concentric display where' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Donut: A concentric display where a row of data forms a segment and each segment corresponds to the total value of the ring. • Line: A representation of a series of data points connected by a line. When multiple variables are in place, the chart may display a trend. • List: An enumeration of data. • Pie: Distribution of data in the form of slices within a circular graph.

cards.question: Page 102 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Each division equates to a portion' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Each division equates to a portion of the total data that corresponds to a certain value. • Multi-area: An extension of the Area chart where multiple sets of data are simultaneously visualized on the same graph. • Multi-line: An extension of the Line chart where multiple sets of data are simultaneously visualized on the same graph. • Progress Chart or Target Chart: A graphical comparison of data to track progress towards the target value. You can perform these tasks in this widget: Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 103 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain '• Configure the chart settings. •' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Configure the chart settings. • Refresh the chart by clicking the refresh icon from the widget title bar. • Export and upload chart configurations to and from a CSV file. • Add and delete chart configurations. • Drill back to M3 where the data for the currently selected chart is available.

cards.question: Page 103 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Prerequisite Before using the widget, you' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Prerequisite Before using the widget, you must have knowledge about the M3 fields, programs, and transactions that you intend to use. Configuring the In-Context Information widget This widget supports user-based configuration only. Click the menu icon ( ) on the widget title bar and select any of these options: a Configure > Create Static Chart: Select to show a chart which does not de

cards.question: Page 103 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'b Configure: Select to show a' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: b Configure: Select to show a new chart which depends on context value. c Edit Chart: Select to edit the configuration of existing charts in the widget. On the General tab, specify this information: Name Specify the name of the chart. Type Select the chart type from the options. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 104 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Screen association To show the widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Screen association To show the widget on a specific M3 program, specify the M3 program, for example, CRS610. This field is not available for static charts. Color picker Select the chart color. On the Data Retrieval tab, specify this information: Note: To display the charts, the selected API program, transaction, and query must be valid. API program Select the M3 program, for example OIS100MI. API transaction Select a transaction, for example Search.Head. Query, Arguments Specify the query or the argument to use to retrieve data. If functional search macros are supported, the values must be enclosed in {}. For example, ITNO:{ITNO} WHLO:{WHLO}. For non-functional search, insert comma between the list of fields and values.

cards.question: Page 104 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'For example, ITNO,{ITNO},WHLO,{WHLO}. See Supported macros' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For example, ITNO,{ITNO},WHLO,{WHLO}. See Supported macros for the input field on page 130. To help in building the correct query or arguments, you can click these icons: • Field Help: Shows the available transaction fields that you can select to include in your query or arguments. The options in bold text are required. For functional search transactions, only the SQRY - Search Query option is available so this tool is more useful for non-functional search transactions. • Show received context keys and values: Shows the list of keys and values that are last received by the widget. Use the list as a reference. • Test API: Opens a dialog box where you can test your query or argument. These options are available: • Query or Arguments: This field shows a copy of your query or arguments. If blank, specify the query or the argument to use to retrieve data.

cards.question: Page 104 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain '• Test API: Click this button' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Test API: Click this button to validate your specified query or arguments. A message is displayed to indicate whether the transaction is valid along with the number of records that are returned by the valid transaction. • Save query upon close: Select this check box to copy your query or arguments to the Query or Arguments field on the Data Retrieval tab. Search Select this check box to indicate that the API transaction is a functional search. If this check box is clear, the Query field becomes Arguments field. On the Data Value tab, specify this information: Value type Select the method of how the data is processed from these options: • Count: Count the number of returned records. • Sum: Summarize the value of a numeric field in the returned records. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 105 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Value field If you are configuring' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Value field If you are configuring a List chart, select the fields to display on the list. If you selected Sum in the Value type field, select the fields to summarize and display on the chart. Group by field Specify the field by which the data is grouped. For example, to create a chart with stopped orders per customer, select CUNO. Formatter - Group by field If applicable to your chart type, select the format of the value in the Group by field field. For example, if you select Year and month, 20230330 becomes March-2023. On the Drillback tab, specify this information: Program Specify the program in M3 from where the data is based. For functional search transactions, a drillback opens the program and performs a functional search.

cards.question: Page 105 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'For non-functional search transactions, a drillback' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For non-functional search transactions, a drillback only opens the program and the data must be manually searched. Sorting order If applicable to your program, select the field by which the data is sorted. View Select which type of view the chart is displayed. On the Settings tab, specify this information: Max records Specify the number of maximum records to display in the chart. Sort by Select a method for sorting the data. If you are using formatters, it is recommended to leave this field blank. Priority Specify a number from to 99, where is the highest priority and is the lowest. In case multiple charts are valid, the chart with the highest priority is displayed first.

cards.question: Page 105 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'When configuring a new chart, the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: When configuring a new chart, the priority is set to by default. Rotate labels Select this check box to show the long labels diagonally to prevent overlap. Use short names when available Select this check box to show a shorter version of the label. For example, January- becomes Jan-2023. Click Save. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 106 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Issue Overview Use this widget to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Issue Overview Use this widget to view the latest summary for your selected issue in 'Issue. Open' (IMS100) using Infor Gen AI. The issue summary usually includes the issue description, actions completed, and actions required. However, the content may still require verification to ensure the accuracy of AI-generated responses. The summary is also saved in M3 as a text block.

cards.question: Page 106 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'However, modification and deletion of the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: However, modification and deletion of the summary can only be done from Issue Management in M3 Portals. See M3 Business Engine User Documentation Library (Cloud) and M3 Portals Library, and then select Issue Management. Another functionality of the widget includes Copy text to clipboard, wherein you can copy the summary and paste it on a separate notepad or word file to check the content in another format. Prerequisites • You must open (IMS100/B) in M3 where customer issues are retrieved before you can use this widget. This widget retrieves context messages that contains an issue number and loads its corresponding issue summary in the text block.

cards.question: Page 106 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'See supported context message in this' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: See supported context message in this document to know the required entity for this widget. • When you use this as a standalone widget in a workspace, you must open the M3 Information Viewer widget where issues are retrieved. Supported context message This widget receives this published inforWidgetContext message: InforIssueHeader - IMNR. APIs used in the widget The Issue Overview widget requires application program interfaces (APIs) that retrieve and display the appropriate data. This table shows the available APIs for this widget: TransactionProgram GetUserDataMNS150MI GetIMS100MI LstIMS101MI LstIMS010MI LstIMS030MI SelectEXPORTMI GetTextIDCRS980MI GetTextIDCRS980MI LstTxtBlocksCRS980MI Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 107 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'TransactionProgram SltTxtBlockCRS980MI Generating an issue summary' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: TransactionProgram SltTxtBlockCRS980MI Generating an issue summary Use this procedure when you have an empty summary on the widget. In M3, click an issue from 'Issue. Open' (IMS100). If used in a workspace, click an issue from the M3 Information Viewer widget. On the widget, click Summarize project <issue number> where <issue number> is a unique ID for the issue patterned after the series type reserved for Issue Management. The summary request might take a while to load the content. Click Save. Apart from what is saved on the widget, the summary is also saved to the project’s text block in M3.

cards.question: Page 107 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Refreshing an issue summary This procedure' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Refreshing an issue summary This procedure is applicable when your project activity contains an existing executive summary. Note: The summary becomes outdated when the generated summary date is older than the issue header date. This situation occurs when the issue is updated, making the generated summary invalid. Click an issue from 'Issue. Open' (IMS100). If used in a workspace, click an issue from the M3 Information Viewer widget. On the Issue Overview widget, click Refresh summary for <issue number> where <issue number> depends on the selected issue for the display. Configuring the Issue Overview widget The cache setting is used to determine if the summary is expired.

cards.question: Page 107 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Expired indicates that the generated summary' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Expired indicates that the generated summary date is older than the date computed based on the configuration settings. For example, if the configuration is set for one week, a generated summary that is more than one week old is considered expired. Click the menu icon on the widget title bar and select Configure Specify this information: Title Click the unlock button and specify a widget title. Summary expiration value (if selected below) Specify a quantity to match with the expiration unit of the issue. For example, specify 10. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 108 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Summary cache expiration unit Select an' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Summary cache expiration unit Select an expiration unit to match with the expiration value. If you specify in the summary expiration value and select minutes in the expiration unit, compute days from the current date to know the validity of the summary. Click Save. Item Creation Use this widget to create an item using a hierarchy-based template item from your workspace. This widget receives information from hierarchy-based template items and prerequisite data must be defined first before using the widget. Perform the prerequisite tasks based on the required data definition as follows: Define the hierarchy ID with appropriate levels, such as Lvl1, Lvl2, Lvl3 etc., in 'Item Hierarchy: Open' (MMS021).

cards.question: Page 108 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Specify a template item in 'Item' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Specify a template item in 'Item. Open' (MMS001) that should be similar with the code of the hierarchy ID provided in (MMS021). Take note that if a template item is defined at Lvl1, the widget will load only the Lv1 information even though further levels are available in the hierarchy master. We recommend that you always define the template item in (MMS001) similar to the hierarchy ID defined for the lowest hierarchy level. For example, if H1 is Lvl1 as hierarchy code in the (MMS021) program, then this ID should also be mapped to lower levels like H1H2 as Level 2, and H1H2H3 as Level 3. If the template item exist for H1 being Lvl1 hierarchy ID, H1H2 or H1H2H3 will not be loaded in the widget.

cards.question: Page 108 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'In this case, you can define' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: In this case, you can define the template item in (MMS001) at the lowest level in the hierarchy, that is, H1H2H3 being the level in the hierarchy ID defined in (MMS021). Prerequisite Before using the widget, you must activate these tables in the M3 Function Search administration: • MITHRY • MITMAS See information about updating table indexes in the M3 Core User and Administration Library (Cloud). Using the Item Creation widget On the Item Creation widget, click New Item. Specify this information: Hierarchy Select a level hierarchy ID from the options defined in (MMS021).
