cards.question: Page 113 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'List of APIs Select from the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: List of APIs Select from the list of APIs. This field enables you to use your own API definition based only on the available APIs. Note: The options are limited to MMS200MI and CMS100MI. Transactions Select a transaction to work with the API. The list of options for this field depends on the selected API. Output Select an output from the list which determines the data that are displayed in the widget. You can only select a maximum of output values and you must always include ITNO in your selection as it is required. Also, the list of options may vary which depends on the selected transaction.

cards.question: Page 113 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Sort Optionally, select the sort option' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Sort Optionally, select the sort option which depends on the output values. Listing search query Optionally, specify a search query that retrieves the required information from the existing records in M3. Get Image query Specify the IDM query definition that returns the available images in the content area of the widget. Click Save. Master Data Text Use the Master Data Text widget to view a text block that includes an image retrieved from IDM, an item number, and the name of your selected item. You can also use this widget to view customer or supplier information, which includes assigned initials and full name. This widget is available as a contextual component in M3 or as a workspace widget. You can use the widget to view key information for these selections: • A selected item from 'Item.

cards.question: Page 113 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (MMS001) • A selected customer' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MMS001) • A selected customer from 'Customer. Open' (CRS610) • A selected supplier from 'Supplier. Open' (CRS620) The information is shown as a text block configured from the T panel of each respective programs. To use the widget, search for an item, customer, or a supplier. The search returns the corresponding text, which is then displayed in the Master Data Text widget. Prerequisite Ensure that either of these widgets are added along with the Master Data Text widget from the widget catalog on your workspace: • Item Details • Customer Contacts Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 114 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain '• Supplier List Supported context messages' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Supplier List Supported context messages This widget receives these published inforBusinessContext and inforWidgetContextmessages: • InforItemMaster • InforCustomerMaster • InforSupplierMaster Supported screens This widget responds to context when you are in the configured screen panel. By default, associated screens are set to (MMS001/B), (CRS610/B), and (CRS620/B). APIs used in the widget This widget requires application program interfaces (APIs) that retrieve and display appropriate data. This table shows details about M3 APIs available in this widget: TransactionProgram GetBasicDataCRS610MI GetBasicDataCRS620MI GetTextIDCRS980MI SltTxtBlockCRS980MI LstTxtBlocksCRS980MI GetMMS200MI See information about testing and running an API and its corresponding transaction in the M3 Core User and Administration Library (Cloud).

cards.question: Page 114 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Configuring the Master Data Text widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Configuring the Master Data Text widget Click the menu icon on the widget title bar and select Configure. Specify this information: Title Specify a widget title. Ensure that the field is unlocked. Image query Specify the IDM query definition that returns the available images in the content area of the widget.

cards.question: Page 114 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Note: You must change this query' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note: You must change this query based on the tenant specific attributes. If more than one Document type is used in IDM for Item image, you can use JOIN as part of the Get Image query. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 115 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Screen Association Provide only the screens' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Screen Association Provide only the screens that retrieve context messages to display text on the widget. Specify a program, a set of programs, or programs with specific panels which comes from M3. By default, associated screens are set to this format: MMS001/B,CRS610/B,CRS620/B or without panel restriction MMS001,CRS610,CRS620. Click Save. M3 Application Messages Use this widget to display the latest messages received in M3. Note: Items that are displayed in this widget are taken from (CRS640). Using this widget The context menu provides options that you can use for each item displayed in the application messages widget. To access the context menu, right-click an item and select your preferred option: • Select Open to go to Infor M3.

cards.question: Page 115 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain '• Select Copy link to clipboard' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Select Copy link to clipboard to open an M3 record. • Select Mark as complete to change the status of the task to complete. When you select this option, a prompt is displayed to confirm the completion. After confirming, the completed task is removed from the widget. In M3, the status field of the record in (CRS420) is automatically set to 50-'Completed'. • Every task in the widget is assigned a color depending on the status: • Blue: signifies that the task is not started yet. • Yellow: signifies that the task is needed for the current date. • Red: signifies that the task is overdue.

cards.question: Page 115 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Configuring the M3 Application Messages widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Configuring the M3 Application Messages widget Click Configure Widget. Specify this information: Title Click the unlock button and specify a widget title. Sort by Click the drop down field and select a sorting option. Sort order Click the drop down field and select an order for sorting the messages. Click Save. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 116 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'M3 Environment Use this widget to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: M3 Environment Use this widget to display information about the current M3 environment and user. You can change the title of this widget as necessary. M3 Information Creator Use this widget to create a new data in M3. You can run this widget as standalone or retrieve data from another widget based on your selected item, customer, or other objects. The retrieved data depends on the entity declared on the widget configuration. You can also integrate this widget with Infor Go to use its scan functionality. Prerequisites • As a prerequisite to Infor Go, you must install and configure the application in your mobile devices. To know the installation and configuration setup, see Infor Go for Android and iOS in the Infor OS User and Administration Documentation Library (Cloud).

cards.question: Page 116 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'When you have configured the widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: When you have configured the widget and the Infor Go application, you can click the camera icon to scan QR codes and get values for a specific item. Although, you must strictly follow this format for the code to be interpreted in the widget: { "FieldId1": "FieldValue1", "FieldId2": "FieldValue2" }. • To require the widget to use context message values, ensure that the M3 Information Viewer or other related widgets that publish InforWidgetContext messages are added to your workspace. In M3, you must start a program that publishes the required InforBusinessContext messages. Configuring the M3 Information Creator widget Follow this widget configuration to use basic API transactions for creating data. Click Configure Widget. Specify this information: Title Click the unlock button and specify a widget title. Configure the M3 API: Program Click the look-up button in this required field.

cards.question: Page 116 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'You can search for M3 programs' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You can search for M3 programs, for example CRS610, and provide a description to filter the results. Click a record from the programs list and click Select. Add Transaction Click the look-up button in this required field. You can search for a transaction, for example AddTool, and provide a description to filter the results. Click a record from the transactions list and click Select. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 117 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Input Fields Click the look-up button' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Input Fields Click the look-up button in this required field. On the data grid, you can perform these tasks: • Select Input to identify input fields to be submitted to the API transaction. • Select Hidden to hide input fields to be submitted to the API transaction. A hidden field can hold a suggested Value which can only be viewed through an API request. Note: The hidden functionality only works if Input is selected. • Change an input field's value in the Value column. • Add an entity in the Value field to indicate the type of context message to be retrieved for a particular record. The value usually follows this format: {<entity>}, for example, {InforCustomerPartyMaster}. If multiple messages are required, you only have to append .id<ID number> for the declared entity, for example, {InforCustomerPartyMaster.id2}. • Optionally, configure a browse field. See Configuring a browse field.

cards.question: Page 117 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain '• Use the View Order column' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Use the View Order column to sort the order of the fields as displayed on the widget. Fields with lower view order are placed first on the top of the widget. For example, if the input fields are set with these values, CUNO=100, CUNM=10, and STAT=50, the widget displays the fields from top to bottom starting from CUNM, STAT and then CUNO. • Use the Modify Field type to override the field type and length of characters that you supply as metadata. You may use these options: • Default: Uses the field type assigned by the system. • String: Uses text characters to specify values. You can also customize the character length. • Numeric: Uses numeric characters to specify values. You can customize the character length. • Date: Transforms the field into a calendar picker so you can select the day, month, and year. • Checkbox: Transforms the field into a checkbox.

cards.question: Page 117 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain '• Change the display labels of' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Change the display labels of input fields through the Modify Description functionality. You can also specify a translation for the new label. For the translated label to reflect, you must set the equivalent language in the user settings of Infor OS. Click Save. Configuring a browse field In the Input fields, click the look-up button. Specify this information: Table Specify a table, for example OCUSMA. Field Override Specify any M3 API Field, for example CONO. Set as Browse Select the check box to set the parameter as browse field. Browse Field Click the Edit button to modify the browse field overrides. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 118 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'DescriptionField Contains M3 API fields, for' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: DescriptionField Contains M3 API fields, for example CONO.Field Override Contains an index of the available data.Parameter List Index Contains M3 table values, for example {} or OCUSMA. This field is auto filled by the Parameter List Index. From Table Contains browse variant values, for example or 01. This field is auto filled by the Parameter List Index. Browse Variant Selecting this check box enables you to specify filter fields.Add a filter View Order Specify a value to specify the order of the input fields for display. Click Save. M3 Information Editor Use this widget to edit existing data in M3.

cards.question: Page 118 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'You can also integrate this widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You can also integrate this widget with Infor Go to use its scan functionality. Prerequisite As a prerequisite to Infor Go, you must install and configure the application in your mobile devices. To know the installation and configuration setup, see Infor Go for Android and iOS in the Infor OS User and Administration Documentation Library (Cloud). When you have configured the widget and the Infor Go application, you can click the camera icon to scan either QR codes or bar codes for these purposes: • To populate a Filter List text box and perform a filter based on the string read by the scanning. For example, if the value of the QR code is 1234567890, the Filter List filters all rows with fields containing the same value. • To populate field values when editing a selected record. For the field values to be populated, you must follow this format: { "FieldId1": "FieldValue1", "Fiel dId2": "FieldValue2" }.

cards.question: Page 118 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Modifying information in this widget Open' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Modifying information in this widget Open the M3 Information Editor widget. Optionally, specify a value or a description in the Search field. Click the available record from the list. The displayed fields are based from the values gathered from the M3 Information Creator. You can modify values in the existing fields, for example Name field. Click Submit. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 119 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Configuring the M3 Information Editor widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Configuring the M3 Information Editor widget Follow this widget configuration to use basic API transactions for updating data. Click Configure Widget. Specify this information: Title Click unlock button and specify a widget title. Automatic Refresh Rate Select a refresh rate minute interval from the list. Specify this information: Program Click the look-up button in this required field. Search for an M3 program, for example CRS610, and provide a description to filter the results. Click a record from the programs list and click Select. Get Transaction Click the look-up button in this required field. You can search for a transaction, for example SearchCustomer, and provide a description to filter the results.

cards.question: Page 119 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Click a record from the transactions' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Click a record from the transactions list and click Select. Input Click the look-up button. On the data grid, you can perform these tasks: • Select Input to identify the input fields to be submitted to the API transaction. • Change the input field's value in the Value column. • Optionally, specify an entity type to require the widget to retrieve business context messages, for example, InforCustomerPartyMaster. And then specify an input in the Value field that uses the entity type enclosed in a bracket. This example shows the format: {InforCustomerPartyMaster}. The same input is reflected in the Id1 context message which you can use as an input field to run the API request. Note: Other supported macros are used in the Value field.

cards.question: Page 119 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'See Supported macros for the input' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: See Supported macros for the input field. Output Click the look-up button in this required field. On the datagrid, you can perform these tasks: • Select Output to identify the output fields to be submitted for the API transaction. • Specify a value under the View Order column to specify the order of the output fields for display, for example CUNO;CUNM;STAT;CUCL. • Change the display labels of columns through the Modify Description functionality. You can also specify a translation for the new label. For the translated label to reflect, you must set the equivalent language in the user settings of Infor OS. • Select a maximum record count. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 120 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Update Transaction Click the look-up button' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Update Transaction Click the look-up button in this required field. You can search for update transactions, for example ChgBasicData and provide a description to filter the results. Click a record from the transactions list and click Select. Update Program Specify an M3 program. When executing an edit API request, the Program value is overridden with this field. Normally, the values for Program and Update Program are similar, for example, CRS610. When Update Program is changed, the widget uses the Update Program value instead to construct the API request. This enables the widget to use other programs for updating records. Editable Fields Click the look-up button in this required field to switch to the Input/Output Parameters page. On the data grid, you can perform these tasks: • Select Input to identify input fields to be submitted to the API transaction.

cards.question: Page 120 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain '• Select Hidden to hide input' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Select Hidden to hide input fields submitted to an API transaction. Note: The hidden functionality only works if Input is selected. • Change the input field's value in the Value column. • Change the display labels of input fields through the Modify Description functionality. You can also specify a translation for the new label. For the translated label to reflect, you have to set the equivalent language in the user settings of Infor OS. • Optionally, configure a browse field. See Configuring a browse field. Click Save. M3 Information Monitor Use this widget to collect multiple information viewers into a single window.

cards.question: Page 120 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'You have the option to configure' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You have the option to configure the view of your information. Additionally, you can right-click a specific monitor and perform any of these actions: • Open the M3 bookmark that is configured in the monitor by selecting Open. • View the details of the records that are counted by the monitor by selecting Drilldown. • Modify the data retrieved by the monitor by selecting Edit. • Create a duplicate of a specific monitor together with the configurations made with it by selecting Copy. • Remove an existing monitor from the widget by selecting Delete. • From a specific record in the drilldown view, you can also perform these actions: • Open the M3 bookmark that is configured in the Output Parameters window by selecting Open. • Copy the bookmark link of the record by selecting Copy to Clipboard. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 121 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Configuring the M3 Information Monitor widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Configuring the M3 Information Monitor widget Follow this widget configuration to use basic API transactions. Click Configure Widget. Specify this information: Title Click the unlock button and specify a widget title. Automatic Refresh Rate Select a refresh rate interval from the drop-down field. Display Select either Default to apply the grid view or Tiles to apply the tiles view for all records. The monitor can display a maximum of one hundred tiles, divided by page navigators. When the maximum display is reached, +Add Monitor is disabled. Click Add Monitor and configure the monitor to be added. Monitor Name Specify the monitor name.

cards.question: Page 121 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Translations Click this option to translate' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Translations Click this option to translate the monitor name into a different language. See Using Translations in M3 Information Monitor widgets. Severity Indication Enable this setting to display the configured indicators for the number of records. If enabled, click Configure. In the Configure Severity Indication window, use the slider to apply a color and set a numerical range for the input to indicate the level of severity. Primary action Select the action to perform when a monitor is clicked: • Open: Performs a drillback to M3 using the configuration details. For example, clicking a monitor directs you to 'Item. Open' (MMS001/B). • Drilldown: Performs a drilldown to the specific data retrieved from M3.

cards.question: Page 121 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'When you click a monitor which' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: When you click a monitor which shows fifteen customers for example, the list of customers with their respective information get displayed in grid view. • No Action: Disables drillback and drilldown. Clicking a monitor does not trigger any action. Max record count Specify the number of records to be displayed for the specific monitor. If the record count exceeds the max record count, a plus sign is added as an indicator. Bookmark Options Click Get Bookmark and select an existing bookmark option Optionally, modify your own bookmark. See Configuring a bookmark. Specify this M3 API information: Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 122 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain '• Select a maximum record count' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Select a maximum record count. • Select the Standard bookmark variant for your output fields with key strings (KSTR): • If Modify Standard Configuration is enabled, you can specify an option, start panel, and focus field for your bookmark. • To use the default key string for the record, disable Modify Standard Configuration. • Select the Configurable bookmark variant to enable a bookmark for a specific record. See Enabling bookmark in a record. Display content as Select a display type from the list. If you select graph, additional fields should be configured. See Configuring a graph. cards.question: Page 123 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'See Adding an API action. Click' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: See Adding an API action. Click OK. Adding an API action Use this procedure to configure an API to create additional action for a specific record. The configured action can be accessed through a context menu when you right-click a record. Note: Performing an action only provides a successful or failed result. When configuring the widget, click + Add Action. Specify this information: Action Name Specify a name for the action to be used for the API. Program Click the look-up button and select a program. Transaction Click the look-up button and select from the available transaction methods.

cards.question: Page 123 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Input Click the look-up button. On' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

For example, updating a supplier status to deactivated, STAT: (CRS620MI/ChgBasicData). Click OK. Using the Copy Monitor functionality Use this functionality to copy an existing monitor without doing a configuration of the widget manually. Click Configure Widget. Click + Add Monitor. Complete your configuration.

cards.question: Page 123 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'See Configuring the M3 Information Monitor' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: See Configuring the M3 Information Monitor widget. Click Save. Right-click your existing widget, and select Copy. Optionally, specify a new title for your widget copy and click OK. All configurations from the source widget are copied to the new widget. You may update the configurations of your widget copy after using this functionality. cards.question: Page 124 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Using Translations in M3 Information Monitor' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Using Translations in M3 Information Monitor widgets Use this procedure to add translations that converts the monitor names to your preferred language. Note: You can only view the change when you set the equivalent language from the user menu of workspaces. On the Edit Monitor dialog, click Translations. On the Translations dialog, click Add Translation to add more languages.

cards.question: Page 124 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Click OK. On the Edit Monitor' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Click OK. On the Edit Monitor dialog, click OK to exit. M3 Information Viewer Use this widget to display an overview of the frequent information that you use in M3. You can run this widget as standalone. However, this widget can also send context messages or retrieve context message data from the another widget, or both simultaneously. The entities on the widget configuration determines the information being sent or retrieved by this widget. Additionally, you can perform these actions: • Click a chart item to display drilldown.

cards.question: Page 127 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Query Specify a bookmark query value' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Query Specify a bookmark query value, for example, use CONO: to view a customer number with the value of 880. You can also add an entity in bookmark queries to support context value using this format: <Field name>:{<context message>} or <Field name>:{<context message>}.id<number>. For example, use CUNO:{InforCustomerPartyMaster} to view all existing customer numbers. Click OK. Chart types These selections are available from the Chart Type field in the graph view settings: • Pie: Distribution of data in the form of "slices" within a circular graph. Each division equates to a portion of the total data that corresponds to a certain value. • Bar: A horizontal comparison of data through the use of columns. • Stacked Bar: Similar to a bar chart except that each column is divided into colored segments wherein a segment represents a single, different value in a data series.

cards.question: Page 127 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain '• Donut: A concentric display where' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Donut: A concentric display where a row of data forms a segment and each segment corresponds to the total value of the ring. • Column: A vertical comparison of data that shares the same category or label. • Stacked Column: Similar to a column chart except that each column is divided into colored segments wherein a segment represents a single, different value in a data series. • Line: A representation of a series of data points connected by a line. When multiple variables are in place, the chart may display a trend. • Area: A display of quantitative data connected by line segments and the area below the line is covered with color. • Count: A numeric display of data wherein a set of digits represent the count or amount of a specific value. Configuring a graph In Display content as, select graph as your display type.

cards.question: Page 127 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Click Configure. In the graph view' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Click Configure. In the graph view settings, specify this information: Chart type Select a chart type from the list. Note: If you select the Stacked Bar and Stacked Column options, the Stack Column drop-down becomes available. Label Column Select a label column from the list. The values are taken from the fields selected in the MI Output Configuration above, step 4. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 128 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Stack Column Select the category that' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Stack Column Select the category that defines several subgroups that are present and compared in all columns. Rotate label Switch this option to rotate the labels to a readable angle. Note: This option is only available to column, line, and area chart types. Display Y-Axis label Switch this option to show the label specified on the Y-Axis Label field. Note: This option is only available to column, line, and area chart types. Y-Axis Label Specify a label to be displayed vertically on the graph. Note: This option is only available to column, line, and area chart types. The label can also be translated into multiple languages.

cards.question: Page 128 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Translations Click this option to configure' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

See details about changing the site default language in Infor OS Portal User Guide. a Click Add Translation. b On theAdd Translation dialog box, specify this information: Language Select your preferred translation for the label. Y-Axis Label Specify the translation to be reflected on the graph. c Click Add. d On the Translation dialog box, click OK.

cards.question: Page 130 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'Supported macros for the input field' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Supported macros for the input field • Infor entities macros The macros for entities uses this format: {<infor_provided_entity_name>}. For example, {InforCustomerPartyMaster}. Take note that the user should also add the same entity name on the entity type column when configuring the input parameter. • Location macros The macros for location uses this format: {<coordinate>, [<no_of_decimals>]} where the value inside the square brackets [] are optional. The types of location macros include: • {longitude} • {latitude} • {latitude, 3} where is optional in this example • Date macros The date macros uses this format: DATE(<integer>), where the value of integer calculates the time frame from the current date. For example: • DATE(-30): calculates date of days behind from the current date • DATE(0): current date • DATE(+30): calculates date of days ahead from the current date Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widge

cards.question: Page 131 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'This list contains the date macros' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This list contains the date macros: • DATE: A day value • WEEK: A date range value for a week • MONTH: A date range value for a month • YEAR: A date range value for a year • PERI4: A period value for a month with four characters in YYMM format • PERI6: A period value for a month with six characters in YYYYMM format • PERY4: A range period value for a year with four characters in YYMM format. For example, TO 2212.

cards.question: Page 131 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain '• PERY6: A range period value' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • PERY6: A range period value for a year with six characters in YYYYMM format • User context macros The macros for user context uses this format: {<user context fields>}. Note: The user context fields are retrieved from the current user records from (MNS150).

cards.question: Page 136 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (CRS610). Right-click a specific customer' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CRS610). Right-click a specific customer and select Change. From the E panel, right-click any blank space and select Bookmarks > Copy to Clipboard. This URL is an example of a copied bookmark: bookmark?program=CRS610&panel=E&tableName=OCUSMA&source=Web&option=2&keys=OKCONO%2C780%2COK CUNO%2CADAMCUST%2B%2B&LogicalId=lid://infor.m3.m3. Some bookmark elements are dynamic. For the provided URL example, you can replace the customer number, that is ADAMCUST, with <CUNO>. We recommend that you update the URL on a notepad to identify dynamic elements conveniently. You can paste the updated URL to this field: bookmark?program=CRS610&panel=E&tableName=OCUSMA&source=Web&option=2&keys=OKCONO%2C780%2COK CUNO%2C<CUNO>%2B%2B&LogicalId=lid://infor.m3.m3. Changing the actual customer number with a generic field redirects the user to a panel with more records other than the only customer record where we copied the raw bookmark.

Project Executive Summary Use this widget to summarize a project recorded in (POS100) using GenAI. An executive summary usually includes the project’s overview, status, performance, budget analysis, suggestions for known issues, and a conclusion. However, the content may still require verification to ensure the accuracy of AI-generated responses. The sections for suggestion and conclusion, for example, are results based on the data gathered from the project activities.

cards.question: Page 147 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'The summary is also saved in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The summary is also saved in M3 as a text block and as a document in Infor Document Management (IDM). Another functionality of the widget includes Copy text to clipboard, wherein you can copy the summary content and paste it on a separate notepad or word file to check the content in another format. Prerequisites • When you use this widget in a workspace, you must open another widget that sends the required entity type that is used to retrieve appropriate data. For example, you may use the M3 Search and List widget to get and display information on project data. • You must open or start a project before you use this in M3. See the M3 Business Engine User Documentation Library (Cloud) and select M3 Project Management. • You are required to configure IDM to enable adding and saving of documents both in IDM and M3. See Infor OS User and Administration Documentation Library (Cloud) for information on in-context applications.

cards.question: Page 147 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain '• Create a mapping of the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Create a mapping of the document ID using XQuery to display only the applicable information in the widget and related information context application. See Infor OS User and Administration Documentation Library (Cloud) to for information on business context mode. • You must add InforProject as an entity type for the IDM Business Context Model. Ensure that UNION /Project_Executive_Summary[@Project_Number = "{id1}"] is included in the XQuery field with consideration that the Project number is set as id1 in the InforProject entity. Supported context message This widget receives this published inforBusinessContext message: InforProject - PROJ. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.question: Page 148 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Generating a project summary Generating an' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Generating a project summary Generating an executive summary is most significant for this widget to fulfill its main purpose. This procedure is applicable when you have an empty summary on the widget. In M3, click a project from ‘Project. Open’ (POS100). If used in a workspace, click a project from the M3 Search and List widget. On the widget, click Summarize project <project number> where <project number> is a unique ID for the project provided manually by the project manager or a series patterned after the project order type. The summary request might take a while to load the content. Optionally, modify the summary from the widget body. Click Save. Apart from what is saved on the widget, the summary is also saved to the project’s text block in M3 and on Infor Document Management (IDM).

cards.question: Page 148 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Note: When you use the Related' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note: When you use the Related Information widget alongside the Project Executive Summary widget, you can check that it generates a document that contains the similar summary saved on IDM. Refreshing a project summary This procedure is applicable when your project activity contains an existing executive summary. Note: The summary becomes outdated when the generated summary date is older than the project header date. This situation occurs when the project is updated, making the generated summary invalid. Right-click an existing project from ‘Project. Open’ (POS100) and select Related > Consists-of. The related option opens the list of available activities for the project. If used in a workspace, click a project from the M3 Search and List widget. Select the project or any activity from the list. After you select from the list, you can view the summary from the in-context widget.

cards.question: Page 148 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'On the Project Executive Summary widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: On the Project Executive Summary widget, click Refresh summary for <project number> where <project number> is a unique ID for the project provided manually by the project manager or a series patterned after the project order type. See the M3 Business Engine User Documentation Library (Cloud) and select M3 Project Management > Project Number. Verifying a project's budget analysis Use this procedure to verify if the declared budget and expenses for every activity are accurate on the summary. This helps the project manager check which activities have enough budget or excessive expenditures. Right-click an existing project from 'Project. Open' (POS100/B) with status or higher and select Related > Budget. In 'Project Budget. Open' (POS110/B1), right-click a budget and select Related > Lines. cards.question: Page 160 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 1: Explain 'Rental Agreement Creation Use this widget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Rental Agreement Creation Use this widget to create rental agreements and quotations for rental equipments. Prerequisite Before using this widget, you must configure these items: • A minimum of one record must be available in CRS099 Product Line.Open. The CRS099MI-LstProductLine enables the retrieval of a record from CRS099, therefore, enables the user to select an appropriate Product Line value where multiples exist and the results of subsequent searches are determined. It is recommended that a Product Line value such as RENTAL is defined in CRS099. • The product line field must be editable on MMS001/E and MMS002/F. Go to CRS040 > Field Control > Change and configure all item types for Rental. • All items to be included in the widget must have the product line field on the Item Master record (MMS001/E and MMS002/F) that is populated with a defined value from the table (CRS099). The value of the product line field is used to create an a

cards.question: Page 160 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Configuring Rental Agreement Creation widget Click' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Configuring Rental Agreement Creation widget Click the menu icon (…) on the widget title bar and select Configure. Specify this information: Rental Depot Select the rental depot. Stock Zone Select the location of the items for rent. Rental Product Line(PDLN) Select the values that relate to the rental items, for example RENTAL. Ensure that the value clearly indicates that it is for rental use only. Default Rental Agreement Order Type Select the default value for the Rental Order type, for example, CO2. Reason Code (ARCC) Select a default reason setting for creating the agreement. Block on zero availability To enable the user to select an item with zero availability and create a rental agreement, leave this field blank.

cards.question: Page 160 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 3: Explain 'If you select Yes, a warning' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

If more than one Document type is used in IDM for Item image, you can use JOIN as part of the Get Image query. Click Save. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | Widgets

cards.goodAnswer: Creating a rental agreement From the list in the widget, open an item. Specify this information: Customer Specify the customer name. If the rental depot that is configured in the widget is different from the default rental depot of the customer, you can select whether to reset to the default or continue with a different rental depot. Address Specify the customer's address from the list. Line Type Select whether the transaction is for rental or sales. Agreement type Select one from these options: • Temporary: A temporary rental agreement • Quotation: A price quote • Agreement: A permanent rental agreement From, To Specify the rental schedule.

If you select Yes, then you can specify the serial number for the generic item. This field is only available if the selected item type corresponds to a generic item type.

cards.question: Page 163 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'If the Enable Infor AI Model' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If the Enable Infor AI Model setting is enabled in the widget configuration, you are presented with additional record checks before the supplier is created. In the Duplicate suppliers window, you can verify the list of duplicate records. The first row always shows the fields on the widget that has input. When you click Export, the input data which uses the first row in the output together with the succeeding duplicate records are downloaded on a CSV file. Note: For innovation support with using this AI technology, contact DLG-TechnologySales@infor.com. After successfully creating the supplier, a message that shows the supplier name and supplier number is displayed on the widget.

cards.question: Page 166 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'The guidelines include the product error' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The guidelines include the product error, possible causes, troubleshooting actions, repair comments, and notes. Based on the product’s historical record of rendered services compiled by the Tailored Service Guidelines widget, GenAI generates the guidelines. However, the content may still require verification to ensure the accuracy of AI-generated responses. The information displayed on the widget is also saved as a record in the text block of ‘Work Order. Open’ (MOS100) in M3. Prerequisite When you use this widget from workspace, ensure that you have an application or widget, such as the M3 Information Viewer, configured to publish the required context messages.

cards.question: Page 168 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Connect Alternate U/M' (MMS015). When you' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Connect Alternate U/M' (MMS015). When you select an item from either the Item Details widget or from M3, you can view details, such as the item number and name, basic UOM, total and basic weight, total and basic volume, net weight, and free cap units. After the summarized details, you can select these tabs, Display and Calculate UOM. The Display tab, which is also the default tab on the widget display, shows the default unit of measure and all the alternate unit of measures that are mapped to your item. Additional details for each unit of measure such as the defined quantity, conversion factor, and gross weight of your selected item are displayed. On the Calculate UOM tab however you can convert the default UOM, for example Kilograms (Kg), to an alternate UOM, for example Pounds (Lb), where UOM conversion is based on the conversion factor between Kg to Lb as defined in (MMS015) and the quantity that you have specified in the widget.

The Unit of Measure Conversion widget retrieves data based on your selected item from the Item Details widget. Note: Workspace is only available in Infor OS Portal. cards.question: Page 171 in 'Widgets' (M3 widgets) - detail 2: Explain 'Prerequisite Configure this widget to assign' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Prerequisite Configure this widget to assign interface templates to be used for the upload. Limitations • We recommend uploading files that do not exceed 20, lines in the widget. Larger files can result in longer wait times and an increased risk of interruptions. • The widget displays a maximum of erroneous rows. When exporting these rows, the limit is 10,000. • Refreshing the browser during the upload interrupts the process. This is an interactive job, and disruption leaves non-uploaded records in program (GLS840). • Keep your browser (Edge, Chrome, etc.) open during processing. Closing the browser window causes job interruptions. Performance may be affected by the settings for batch jobs if multiple divisions are running simultaneously.

The file is saved on your local machine. c Update the file based on the required values for each column and save the updated file. d Click Upload file, browse to your updated CSV file, and click Open. e Click Next. cards.question: Page 181 in 'H5 Scripts' (M3_H5_CalculatePriceEvolution) - detail 3: Explain 'The script adds three columns where' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The script adds three columns where you can view the price from the previous purchase agreement, the previous purchase agreement number, and the price increase in percentage for the latest agreement. Additionally, the script creates a check box that, when clicked, filters only the records that are greater than the specified argument or the price evolution in percentage. Purchase agreements are retrieved based on these rules: • Records with similar agreement types • Records with similar group identities • Line status that is set between twenty to ninety • Records with the closest from-date agreement in the highest priority Prerequisites Before adding a script, you must activate these tables in the M3 Function Search administration: • MPAGRL • MPAGRH • MPAGRP See information about updating table indexes in the M3 Core User and Administration Library (Cloud). Open' (CRS111). The type of email address that is searched for is based on these tables and the fields following this sequence: • CUNO: "01", (Customer) • HLDR: "01", (Customer) • SUNO: "02", (Supplier) • PYNO: "11", (Payer (customer)) • SPYN: "14", (Payee (supplier)) • EMNO: "03", (Employee) • RESP: "04", (User) • BUYE: "04", (User) • SMCD: "04", (User) • USID: "04" (User) If the field CUNO is present for example, (CRS111) is queried for the email indicated in type "01", (Customer). If no email address is found but the field SUNO also exists in the list, (CRS111) is queried again with the email indicated in type “02” (Supplier) instead

cards.question: Page 198 in 'H5 Scripts' (M3_H5_IDM_DocumentIndicator) - detail 2: Explain 'An argument usually uses these values' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: An argument usually uses these values that are separated by commas: <Specific field from data grid>, <Document type (tenant-specific) in IDM >, <Document attribute (tenant-specific and that depends on the Document type) where Field value is saved>, <ION Endpoint URL> • The first argument pertains to the field in the data grid. The simplest way to get the name of the field is to right-click the column and click Add to Search and take the value that gets displayed in Search (IES Textbox), minus the colon and add that as the first script argument. • The second and third arguments are the type and attribute of the IDM document. This information can be found by checking on the IDM for a specific attachment. • The fourth argument can be easily obtained by taking the Endpoint URL from Infor ION API.

cards.question: Page 216 in 'H5 SDK application' (Product Level Feature Management) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 5: H5 SDK application M3' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 5: H5 SDK application M3 H5 provides an HTML 5-based user interface for accessing work-related information and applications from the M3 business system. To access the application, from M3 navigation menu, select Administration Tools > H5 Administration > Applications tab and click the link of the installed application. For Product Level Feature Management application, refer to this information: This table shows the SDK applications and the paths to access: PathName /mne/apps/infor/m3_product_level_feature_man- agement/ infor/m3_product_level_feature_management /mne/apps/m3_data_comparison_across_tenants/m3_data_comparison_across_tenants Product Level Feature Management You can use the H5 SDK Product Level Feature Management application to view, compare, monitor, and update feature activation state across several tenants in M3. The feature activation is managed through 'Feature List Open' (CMS975) program.

cards.question: Page 216 in 'H5 SDK application' (Product Level Feature Management) - detail 2: Explain 'ION API suite configuration Before using' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: ION API suite configuration Before using the H5 SDK Product Level Feature Management application, you must set up the ION API suite to get monitored tenants. Perform the configuration in this order: Download credentials for the ION API bridge. See Downloading credentials for the ION API bridge on page 217. Create the ION API suite.

cards.question: Page 216 in 'H5 SDK application' (Product Level Feature Management) - detail 3: Explain 'See Creating the ION API suite' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

cards.question: Page 217 in 'H5 SDK application' (Product Level Feature Management) - detail 1: Explain 'Downloading credentials for the ION API' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Downloading credentials for the ION API bridge Use this procedure to create the bridge between the main tenant, which is the lower tenant such as DEV and the monitored tenants, which are the higher tenants such as TST and PROD. Sign in to the main tenant and perform these steps: a In the Applications, select OS > API Gateway. b Select Configuration > General Settings. c In the ION API JWK Management section, click the Add icon. d Download the added JWK key. Sign in to the monitored tenant and perform these steps: a In the Applications, select OS > API Gateway. b Select Authorized Apps. c Click the Add icon.

cards.question: Page 217 in 'H5 SDK application' (Product Level Feature Management) - detail 2: Explain 'd Specify this information: Name Specify' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: d Specify this information: Name Specify the main tenant name. For example, Tenant_Master - Authorization Type Select Backend Service. Description Provide a description. For example, API Bridge. Use Bridge Authentication Select this option. User Impersonation Select this option. ID Translation Select this option. e Click Upload Public Key and upload the downloaded JWK key from the main tenant.

cards.question: Page 217 in 'H5 SDK application' (Product Level Feature Management) - detail 3: Explain 'f Click Save. g Click Download' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: f Click Save. g Click Download Credentials. h Select Create Service Account. i Specify this information: Select the User Management property for ID translation Select User Principal Name (UPN). j Click DOWNLOAD. Creating the ION API suite Use this procedure to define monitored tenants as target endpoints. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | H5 SDK application

cards.question: Page 218 in 'H5 SDK application' (Product Level Feature Management) - detail 1: Explain 'Sign in to the monitored tenant' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Sign in to the monitored tenant and perform these steps: a In the Applications, select OS > API Gateway > Available APIs. b Click Infor M3. c In the Endpoint column, search M3/m3api-rest/v2/execute. d In the Documentation column, click the File icon. e In the OperationID column, search CMS975MI. f In the Documentation column, click the File icon. Copy the Endpoint and Base URL values to use for the Target Endpoint URL value in the next step. Sign in to the main tenant and perform these steps: a In the Applications, select OS > API Gateway > Available APIs. b Click +Add c Click + Create New d Specify this information: Application Name Provide a name for the application.

cards.question: Page 218 in 'H5 SDK application' (Product Level Feature Management) - detail 2: Explain 'For example, Prod-Level Feature Management. API' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For example, Prod-Level Feature Management. API Context Specify M3EQP. This context is used to identify the ION API suite. Suite Name Provide a name for the suite. For example, Prod-Level Feature Management. Suite Icon To select an icon, click Choose Icon. To upload an image from your local drive, click Upload. Description Provide a description. For example, Feature Status Monitored Tenants Tenant_MonitoredA and Tenant_MonitoredB.

cards.question: Page 218 in 'H5 SDK application' (Product Level Feature Management) - detail 3: Explain 'e In the Target Endpoints tab' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: e In the Target Endpoints tab, click the Add icon. f Specify this information: Target Endpoint URL Specify the combination of the Endpoint and Base URL that you have created in the previous step. Follow this format: https://host/MonitoredTenant/M3/m3api-rest/v2/execute/CMS975MI Example: https://mingle-cqa-ionapi.cqa.inforcloudsuite.com/TENANT_ID/M3/m3api-rest/v2/execute/ CMS975MI Note: Ensure that there is v2 before /execute. Target Endpoint Description Provide a description. For example, Tenant_MonitoredA - CMS975 Bridge. Proxy Context Specify the proxy context in this format: Bridge/Tenant_Name/CMS975MI, where the Tenant_Name is the tenant identification to show in the SDK. Infor CloudSuite Food and Beverage Industry Content User Guide | H5 SDK application

The applications are reached through the M3 menu, through Infor OS widgets, as shortcuts, and more.

Customer Order Hub Customer Order Hub is an M3 Experience Designer Application that enables users in various roles to manage customer orders. Managing orders in M3 requires users to navigate multiple programs and panels, which may result in inefficiencies and a higher risk of errors. The application consolidates several M3 programs into a single interface, which simplifies customer order management and enhances productivity. Users can view, edit, and create order headers and lines, manage delivery details, view order and supply chain information, reschedule and confirm orders, and print invoices. The application also supports conditional styling and integrates with H5, providing quick links for actions such as copying orders or modifying delivery addresses. cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 7: Analytics for CloudSuite Food and Beverage M3 Analytics M3 Analytics for Infor M3 Cloud and relevant CloudSuites is a standard content product that provides a unique value proposition for the M3 industry CloudSuite customers by embedding cloud-based, industry-specific analytics content at the core of the CloudSuite. Using this solution, you can access business-critical insight across the industry CloudSuite. M3 Analytics is designed as a starter pack to facilitate the implementation of a BI strategy within an M3 industry CloudSuite.
